title: randia brandisii
intro: Randia brandisii, also known as the Mountain Wild Orange or Randia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to the tropical regions of the Himalayas, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. Traditionally, Randia brandisii is prepared as a herbal tea or decoction, and it is also used to make extracts and tinctures for various health benefits, including digestive health and immune system support.
